{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52113","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52113/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52113/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52113/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52113","id":1986859758,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM52bRLu","number":52113,"title":"Type signature abbreviation in method error sometimes hides the information you need to know why it doesn't match","user":{"login":"oxinabox","id":5127634,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxMjc2MzQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5127634?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox","html_url":"https://github.com/oxinabox","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-11-10T04:32:45Z","updated_at":"2023-11-10T04:32:45Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Consider for example:
```julia
struct Foo{A, B, C} end
bar(::Foo{Tuple{Int}}) = 1
bar(::Foo{Tuple{Float64}}) = 1

bar(Foo{Tuple{Char,Int,Char,Char,Char,Char,Char,Char,Char,Char,Int, Char,Int,Char,Char,Char,Char,Char,Char,Char,Char,Int, Char,Int,Char,Char,Char,Char,Char,Char,Char,Char,Int, Char}, Dict{Int, Dict{Int,  Dict{Int,  Dict{Int,  Dict{Int,  Dict{Int,  Dict{Int,  Dict{Int, Int}}}}}}}}, Dict{Int, Dict{Int, Dict{Int, Int}}}}())
```
This is a method error becuase the first arguement is some giant tuple mixing `Char` and `Int`,
and there are only methods for if it was a 1-tuple of `Int`, or of `Float64`.
The key info you need to find what has gone wrong in your code is that first type parameter.
However, because of type abbreviation in the display of method errors, you do not always see that.
In this case the error is 
```julia
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching bar(::Foo{Tuple{…}, Dict{…}, Dict{…}})

Closest candidates are:
  bar(::Foo{Tuple{Float64}})
   @ Main REPL[3]:1
  bar(::Foo{Tuple{Int64}})
   @ Main REPL[2]:1
```
We can't tell what is wrong with the `Tuple` type param, since its parameters are hidden.
(`show(err)` does make this display fully as you would hope. in my real world example though `show(err)` resulted in enough text being displayed from my stacktrace that my terminal started glitching)


As I understand it, the logic in type-signature abbreviation is such that any type parameters that are required to hit the particular dispatch are shown.
That makes sense for display in stacktraces -- you can know why a particular path was followed.
But that logic does not work for `MethodError`. For method error we want to see enough info to know why no existing method was hit.

I propose that, for now, we should not abbreviate type signatures in method errors,
until we can work out suitable logic for their abbreviation.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52113/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/52113/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3713","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3713/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3713/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3713/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3713","id":2014246606,"node_id":"I_kwDOBOhtSc54DvbO","number":3713,"title":"Resolve should notice when you are being version bound to require something not released yet","user":{"login":"oxinabox","id":5127634,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxMjc2MzQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5127634?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox","html_url":"https://github.com/oxinabox","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-11-28T11:40:03Z","updated_at":"2023-11-28T11:40:03Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":" If my registry is out of date, but I close a project that someone else has been working on (perhaps with a manifest),
 then I can have a (direct or indirect) dependency that `resolve` thinks it can't satisfy due to it being a larger version number than anything in the registry. 

For example 
 ```
(MyProject) pkg> resolve
ERROR: Unsatisfiable requirements detected for package MathTeXEngine [0a4f8689]:
 MathTeXEngine [0a4f8689] log:
 ├─possible versions are: 0.1.0 - 0.5.6 or uninstalled
 └─restricted to versions 0.5.7 by an explicit requirement — no versions left
 ```

The solution in this case is to update the registry, either directly via `registry update` or via something like `up` which will trigger an update.
Pkg could notice this and prompt you to update registry,
that would be clearer than this error.

Technically there are 4 ways I know this can occur

1. Out of date registry (as described above)
2. Direct dependency, which is supposed to be `dev`ed to a version that has the update in its Project.toml (love that feature that that works)
3. the release has been made but it is in a registry you don't have installed
4. there was a mistake in the `[compat]` section of the Project.toml

scriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DilumAluthge/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DilumAluthge/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DilumAluthge/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/DilumAluthge/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"open_issues":9,"closed_issues":8,"state":"open","created_at":"2022-08-25T21:39:30Z","updated_at":"2023-10-13T13:27:06Z","due_on":null,"closed_at":null},"comments":6,"created_at":"2023-10-12T18:52:51Z","updated_at":"2023-10-16T08:00:12Z","closed_at":"2023-10-13T13:27:05Z","author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"It seems that there was a breaking change introduced somewhere between Julia 1.11.0-DEV.550 (working) and 1.11.0-DEV.555 (failing) which caused my code depending on the MD5.jl library to fail (and is still failing under today's nightly, namely 1.11.0-DEV.643). Since neither the MD5.jl codebase nor SHA.jl (which the former depends on) have been touched in a while, the issue seems to be coming from a change in Julia itself.

MWE:

~~~
julia> using MD5

julia> md5("test")
ERROR: type MD5_CTX has no field used
Stacktrace:
 [1] getproperty
   @ Base ./Base.jl:37 [inlined]
 [2] update!(context::MD5.MD5_CTX, data::Base.CodeUnits{UInt8, String}, datalen::Int64)
   @ SHA ~/julia-8a847d520c/share/julia/stdlib/v1.11/SHA/src/common.jl:20
 [3] update!(context::MD5.MD5_CTX, data::Base.CodeUnits{UInt8, String})
   @ SHA ~/julia-8a847d520c/share/julia/stdlib/v1.11/SHA/src/common.jl:20
 [4] md5(data::Base.CodeUnits{UInt8, String})
   @ MD5 ~/.julia/packages/MD5/H5Cdd/src/MD5.jl:18
 [5] md5(str::String)
   @ MD5 ~/.julia/packages/MD5/H5Cdd/src/MD5.jl:24
 [6] top-level scope
   @ REPL[1]:1

julia> versioninfo()
Julia Version 1.11.0-DEV.643
Commit 8a847d520c2 (2023-10-12 15:02 UTC)
Build Info:
  Official https://julialang.org/ release
Platform Info:
  OS: Linux (x86_64-linux-gnu)
  CPU: 24 × Intel(R) Xeon(R) Silver 4310 CPU @ 2.10GHz
  WORD_SIZE: 64
  LLVM: libLLVM-15.0.7 (ORCJIT, icelake-server)
  Threads: 1 on 24 virtual cores
Environment:
  JULIA_PKG_USE_CLI_GIT = true
~~~

The above example was run under Red Hat 8.8; I get the same error on a different machine (macOS) as well.

Original bug report (where it was recommended that I file the bug here instead):
- https://github.com/JuliaCrypto/MD5.jl/issues/11

 But we would need to get rid of whole recursive thing. Not just transpose.

```julia
# Get the fully-qualified name of a module.
function module_fqn(name::Symbol)
    fqn = fullname(Base.root_module(name))
    return join(fqn, '.')
end
```

This doesn't work as
```
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching root_module(::Symbol)

Closest candidates are:
  root_module(::Module, ::Symbol)
   @ Base loading.jl:1873
  root_module(::Base.PkgId)
   @ Base loading.jl:1872
```

Presumably at some point `root_module` changed and the docs were not updated","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/51166/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/51166/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

```julia
julia> @which foo(1; a=1)
kwcall(::Any, ::typeof(foo), x)
     @ Main REPL[1]:1

But if you write a function with keyword arguments and call `@which` on it, it is reported as the method being hit.
though it does get the file/line number  correct for the user facing method.

```julia
julia> @which foo(1; a=1)
kwcall(::Any, ::typeof(foo), x)
     @ Main REPL[1]:1
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/51263/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/51263/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1715237833","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/51263#issuecomment-1715237833","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/51263","id":1715237833,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5mPHPJ","user":{"login":"oxinabox","id":5127634,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxMjc2MzQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5127634?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox","html_url":"https://github.com/oxinabox","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-09-12T08:22:56Z","updated_at":"2023-09-12T08:22:56Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"`@which` in InteractiveUtils. It's for interactive use by the user.
(`Base.which` on the other hand maybe we need to be more careful with.)

> What method should be shown instead?

I see no reason not to just show the signature of the method the user would hit.


So considering:
```julia
julia> foo(x; kwargs...) = x
foo (generic function with 1 method)

julia> @which foo(1)
foo(x; kwargs...)
     @ Main REPL[5]:1

julia> @which foo(1; a=1)
kwcall(::NamedTuple, ::typeof(foo), x)
     @ Main REPL[5]:1
```
we can have both of them show the same
```
foo(x; kwargs...)
     @ Main REPL[5]:1
```


This, for reference is the special case that would need to be changed:
https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/5d82d8095042935be0eb044259098e0d7c695922/stdlib/InteractiveUtils/src/macros.jl#L96C1-L103

We could go further and change how `kwcall` is printed everywhere by special casing it in the `method_show` code.  Which might be nice, if we wanted to also hide it in stack-traces.
But seems out of scope for this issue.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1715237833/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLSensitivity.jl/issues/855","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLSensitivity.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLSensitivity.jl/issues/855/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLSensitivity.jl/issues/855/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLSensitivity.jl/issues/855/events","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLSensitivity.jl/issues/855","id":1828077846,"node_id":"I_kwDOBFTIes5s9kEW","number":855,"title":"Function-based ChainRules rule transferring to Enzyme rules","user":{"login":"ChrisRackauckas","id":1814174,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE4MTQxNzQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1814174?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas","html_url":"https://github.com/ChrisRackauckas","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2023-07-30T20:26:14Z","updated_at":"2023-08-04T06:40:16Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"As mentioned in https://github.com/EnzymeAD/Enzyme.jl/issues/805#issuecomment-1605560557

> Define a macro that defines the Enzyme rules for a given function based on the ChainRules. For example, @ruletransfer conv(x)

`@ruletransfer conv(x)` would define an EnzymeRule that naively calls a ChainRules.jl rule for all of the activity types. This would then serve as a suboptimal fallback that can be applied on a single function basis. The purpose of this macro would be to make it easier to setup the adoption of Zygote throughout the deep learning community by allowing any Zygote rule to trivially port to Enzyme, making Enzyme at least as good as Zygote as quickly as possible. The reason to do it on a function by function basis is that not all rules are necessary, with the main ones being the ones over cudnn kernels. They should of course be replaced with lower level rules that more optimize memory usage as needed, but I think such a simple macro would be a great first step.

This means the rule transfer can be pretty naïve. It could just call the ChainRule and then `realout .= chainrulesout` , i.e. many ChainRules rules will allocate the output while Enzyme can in theory omit the extra allocation, but for the macro it can ignore such optimizations and just ensure it hits coverage first. {"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLSensitivity.jl/issues/858","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLSensitivity.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLSensitivity.jl/issues/858/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLSensitivity.jl/issues/858/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLSensitivity.jl/issues/858/events","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLSensitivity.jl/issues/858","id":1836178027,"node_id":"I_kwDOBFTIes5tcdpr","number":858,"title":"DAEProblem support","user":{"login":"oxinabox","id":5127634,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxMjc2MzQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5127634?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox","html_url":"https://github.com/oxinabox","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-08-04T06:55:42Z","updated_at":"2023-08-04T06:55:42Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"The docs say it supports

> Differential algebraic equations (DAEs) (`DAEProblem` and `ODEProblem` with mass matrices)

but in the DAE example there is only one for the `ODEProblem` with mass matrix.
https://docs.sciml.ai/SciMLSensitivity/stable/examples/dae/physical_constraints/

Speaking to @ChrisRackauckas he says `DAEProblem` isn't supported.

I don't think this is a huge deal since can always reduce a DAE into an `ODEProblem` with a mass matrix
but for some problems the DAEProblem with IDA is significantly faster than the next best ODE Solver with an ODEProblem that we have found.

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLSensitivity.jl/pulls/comments/1288288660","pull_request_review_id":1569252456,"id":1288288660,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOBFTIes5MybmU","diff_hunk":"@@ -227,3 +227,24 @@ p = [1.5, 1.0, 3.0, 1.0]
     (0.0,
         10.0),
     p)
+
+# Make sure original jac is actually called if it is passed and autodiff is fully off
+jac_call_count = 0
+function jac_with_count(J, u, p, t)
+    global jac_call_count
+    jac_call_count+=1
+    (x, y, a, b, c) = (u[1], u[2], p[1], p[2], p[3])
+    J[1, 1] = a + y * b * -1
+    J[2, 1] = t * y
+    J[1, 2] = b * x * -1
+    J[2, 2] = t * c * -1 + t * x
+end
+
+f = ODEFunction(fb, jac = jac_with_count, paramjac = paramjac)
+p = [1.5, 1.0, 3.0]
+absolutely_no_ad_sensealg = ForwardSensitivity(autodiff=false, autojacvec=false, autojacmat=false)
+prob = ODEForwardSensitivityProblem(f, [1.0; 1.0], (0.0, 10.0), p, absolutely_no_ad_sensealg)
+@test SciMLSensitivity.has_original_jac(prob.f)
+@assert jac_call_count == 0
+solve(prob, Tsit5(), abstol = 1e-14, reltol = 1e-14)","path":"test/forward.jl","commit_id":"1d0ce091f49435a50a670435e204cead19d4994a","original_commit_id":"1d0ce091f49435a50a670435e204cead19d4994a","user":{"login":"oxinabox","id":5127634,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxMjc2MzQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5127634?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox","html_url":"https://github.com/oxinabox","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This actually errors, without this change.

Preserving them is largely a matter of working out when the versions of them for the extended system of equations is, given that one already has them for the initial system of equations.

They are passed onward in this line of code: https://github.com/SciML/SciMLSensitivity.jl/blob/6cd27442e6ecf6e5d57b04c9f809c21e78ec6e96/src/forward_sensitivity.jl#L411-L417
Each nothing is something that is missing but not all of them actually matter.

I am listing the ones I have run into that actually cause some (mostly minor) issues here, so we can think about fixing them.

 - [ ] `tgrad`
 - [ ] `jac_prototype`


These are probably good small tasks for people new to the package who are familiar with the math.
A nice point of not totally trivial but not so hard. And not really subject to much design debate.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLSensitivity.jl/issues/886/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLSensitivity.jl/issues/886/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLSensitivity.jl/issues/888","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLSensitivity.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLSensitivity.jl/issues/888/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLSensitivity.jl/issues/888/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLSensitivity.jl/issues/888/events","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLSensitivity.jl/issues/888","id":1871002962,"node_id":"I_kwDOBFTIes5vhT1S","number":888,"title":"sol[var] etc observations do not work on ODEForwardSensitivityProblem","user":{"login":"oxinabox","id":5127634,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxMjc2MzQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5127634?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox","html_url":"https://github.com/oxinabox","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-08-29T06:49:20Z","updated_at":"2023-08-29T06:49:20Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"`SciMLBase.getobserved(prob)` where `prob` was constructed via `ODEForwardSensitivityProblem`
always returns `DEFAULT_OBSERVED` -- which errors if anyone tries to read anything from it.
Even if the original problem did have observations.

My hack around this is:
```julia
function Base.getproperty(f::ODEForwardSensitivityFunction, name::Symbol)
    name == :observed && return f.f.observed
    return getfield(f, name)
end
Base.hasproperty(f::ODEForwardSensitivityFunction, name::Symbol) = (name == :observed) || hasfield(f, name)
SciMLBase.has_observed(f::ODEForwardSensitivityFunction) = SciMLBase.has_observed(f.f)
```

but possibly we should instead be passing it onwards when we make the `ODEProblem` that `ODEForwardSensitivityProblem`,
rather than relying on `getproperty` overloads.

always returns `DEFAULT_OBSERVED` -- which errors if anyone tries to read anything from it.
Even if the original problem did have observations.

My hack around this is:
```julia
function Base.getproperty(f::ODEForwardSensitivityFunction, name::Symbol)
    name == :observed && return f.f.observed
    return getfield(f, name)
end
Base.hasproperty(f::ODEForwardSensitivityFunction, name::Symbol) = (name == :observed) || hasfield(f, name)
SciMLBase.has_observed(f::ODEForwardSensitivityFunction) = SciMLBase.has_observed(f.f)
```

but possibly we should instead be passing it onwards when we make the `ODEProblem` that `ODEForwardSensitivityProblem`,
rather than relying on `getproperty` overloads.

I *think* this is correct as the equations and variables in the expanded system of equations are all placed *after* the original ones. Right?

requests"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2023-05-01T15:04:29Z","updated_at":"2023-07-18T16:46:07Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"There should be a way to create deprecation warnings so that If I use a package that has those deprecated calls, I don't get a warning but if I write code that uses those calls (script, repl, or `]dev`ing a package) I do get a warning.

_Originally posted by @oxinabox in https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47150#issuecomment-1529283166_
            ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49583/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49583/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1640575993","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49583#issuecomment-1640575993","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49583","id":1640575993,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5hyTP5","user":{"login":"oxinabox","id":5127634,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxMjc2MzQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5127634?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox","html_url":"https://github.com/oxinabox","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-07-18T16:46:07Z","updated_at":"2023-07-18T16:46:07Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"So the problem with my solution above is `stacktrace()` is incredibly expensive to call.
It takes about 40ms.
For comparison, just showing the log message (which takes too long to put in inner loops) takes 10us.

An alternative solution is to look at what packages are your direct dependencies.
We can build a set of them at start-up time or when they are loaded etc, or the first time a log message is printed
Then we just need to check at `shouldlog` time for if it has `group=:depwarn` and if `_module` is in this set. Which takes about 10ns.

The downside of this solution is it will give false positives.
If my active enviroment depends on `A` and `B`,  and then `B` also depends on `A`,
and I call some method from `B` which calls a deprecated method from `A` we would still print a warning. But maybe that's acceptable.
Or we could take false negatives instead and only print if it is a package that is *only* a direct dependency. But that seems worse.
","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1640575993/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

reverts commit 24d526871d5203317fa17f93f9a4ef9fb34e8632.
undoing #44358

I propose backporting this PR to bring the operation back in 1.9.1, and holding off #44358 til julia 2.0 where matching changes can be made as a whole set. cc: @antoine-levitt

#44358 was originally made with the assumption that noone was using this feature except in error.
Which was checked using PkgEval.
This was incorrect, and the check for some reason or another missed or skipped over ChainRules.jl.
This broke ChainRules.jl for anyone using reverse mode AD to go through `/` or `\`.

The use in ChainRules.jl is intentional and is required to achieve generality between different types.
Quoting @sethaxen  on slack

> This seems to be a direct consequence of how c = transpose(a)*b for vectors a and b produces a scalar, while c = permutedims(a)*b produces a vector. I'd argue the first case is convenient but wrong, but if we accept it, then since c/b is just the linear solve for a, then /(::Number, AbstractVector{<:Number}) should be supported.

The trivial fix to make ChainRules work again in 1.9.0 (https://github.com/JuliaDiff/ChainRules.jl/pull/718) is uglier, less performant and less numerically good.

I think we do not need to discuss if the feature in #44358 is desirable or not in this PR. 
Merely if it was too breaking or not.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49915/reactions","total_count":4,"+1":3,"-1":1,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49915/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1656300516","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49915#issuecomment-1656300516","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49915","id":1656300516,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5iuSPk","user":{"login":"oxinabox","id":5127634,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxMjc2MzQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5127634?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox","html_url":"https://github.com/oxinabox","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-07-28T20:31:49Z","updated_at":"2023-07-28T20:31:49Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Can we add a Forget-me-not","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1656300516/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

computation"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":16,"created_at":"2019-11-19T18:48:01Z","updated_at":"2023-07-28T21:13:14Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/33892","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/33892","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/33892.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/33892.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Once upon a time, there was a young julia user first getting started with parallelism.
And she found it fearsomely slow.
And so she did investigate, and she did illuminate upon her issue.
Her closures, they were being reserialized again and again.
And so this young woman, she openned an issue #16345.
Lo and behold, a noble soul did come and resolve it,
by making the glorious `CachingPool()` in #16808.

3 long years a later this julia user did bravely return to the world of parallism, with many battle worn scars.
and once more she did face the demon that is `pmap` over closures.
But to her folly, she felt no fear, for she believed the demon to be crippled and chained by the glorious `CachingPool`.
Fearlessly, she threw his closure over 2GB of data into the maw of the demon `pmap`.
But alas, alas indeed, she was wrong.
The demon remained unbound, and it slew her, and slew her again.
100 times did it slay her for 101 items was the user iterating upon. 
For the glorious chains of the the `CachingPool()` remains unused, left aside in the users tool chest, have `vec` which is a short-hand for `reshape(x, Val(1))`.
I propose that we should have `mat` as well, as a shorthand for `reshape(x,Val(2))`.

Obviously `vec` is the most common case of reshaping,
but I suggest that `mat` comes in second, and is much more common than the general `reshape`.
In particular for converting a Vector into a single column matrix.
Very common for interfacing with various libraries.

My old trick for that was to double transpose it (`x''`), but since as of 0.6 we take vector transpose seriously, that won't work. (Plus this way is nonallocating).

(#44358)"","user":{"login":"oxinabox","id":5127634,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxMjc2MzQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5127634?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox","html_url":"https://github.com/oxinabox","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":20,"created_at":"2023-05-22T04:09:19Z","updated_at":"2023-06-02T12:26:03Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/49915","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49915","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49915.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49915.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"This reverts commit 24d526871d5203317fa17f93f9a4ef9fb34e8632.
undoing #44358

I propose backporting this PR to bring the operation back in 1.9.1, and holding off #44358 til julia 2.0 where matching changes can be made as a whole set. cc: @antoine-levitt

#44358 was originally made with the assumption that noone was using this feature except in error.
Which was checked using PkgEval.
This was incorrect, and the check for some reason or another missed or skipped over ChainRules.jl.
This broke ChainRules.jl for anyone using reverse mode AD to go through `/` or `\`.

The use in ChainRules.jl is intentional and is required to achieve generality between different types.
Quoting @sethaxen  on slack

> This seems to be a direct consequence of how c = transpose(a)*b for vectors a and b produces a scalar, while c = permutedims(a)*b produces a vector. I'd argue the first case is convenient but wrong, but if we accept it, then since c/b is just the linear solve for a, then /(::Number, AbstractVector{<:Number}) should be supported.

The trivial fix to make ChainRules work again in 1.9.0 (https://github.com/JuliaDiff/ChainRules.jl/pull/718) is uglier, less performant and less numerically good.

I think we do not need to discuss if the feature in #44358 is desirable or not in this PR. 
Merely if it was too breaking or not.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49915/reactions","total_count":4,"+1":3,"-1":1,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49915/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1573654534","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49915#issuecomment-1573654534","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49915","id":1573654534,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5dzBAG","user":{"login":"oxinabox","id":5127634,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxMjc2MzQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5127634?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox","html_url":"https://github.com/oxinabox","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-06-02T12:26:03Z","updated_at":"2023-06-02T12:26:03Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Can we schedule this in to be talked about at Triage on the 23rd?

Equations"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2020-04-15T05:09:02Z","updated_at":"2023-06-09T11:48:40Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"MWE:

```julia
using OrdinaryDiffEq
tspan = (0.0,1.0)
u0 = zeros(1)
u0[1] = 1/2

function residual(res,du,u,p,t)
  res[1] = - 1.01u[1] + du[1]
end

function jacobian(jac,du,u,p,gamma,t)
  jac[1,1] = gamma - 1.01
  nothing
end

f_iip = DAEFunction{true}(residual;jac=jacobian)
prob_iip = DAEProblem{true}(f_iip,u0,u0,tspan)
sol_iip = solve(prob_iip, DImplicitEuler(), reltol=1e-8, abstol=1e-8)
sol_iip = solve(prob_iip, DABDF2(), reltol=1e-8, abstol=1e-8)
```

The issue is threefold. 

1. The Jacobian references `duprev` which doesn't exist. This should be `fsalfirst`
2. `fsalfirst` isn't defined in the first step, so that will be undefined until the first Jacobian is calculated, which gives a causality issue...
3. `jac` for a DAE is actually the W-matrix, so that needs to move to the `calc_W!` section.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/issues/1109/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/issues/1109/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/issues/comments/1584449577","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/issues/1109#issuecomment-1584449577","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/OrdinaryDiffEq.jl/issues/1109","id":1584449577,"node_id":"IC_kwDOBEVPe85ecMgp","user":{"login":"oxinabox","id":5127634,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxMjc2MzQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5127634?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox","html_url":"https://github.com/oxinabox","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-06-09T11:48:40Z","updated_at":"2023-06-09T11:48:40Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"I am just adding the error message that the above gives, so I can find this again with search

errors","user":{"login":"Keno","id":1291671,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEyOTE2NzE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1291671?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno","html_url":"https://github.com/Keno","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Keno/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"The _new_NamedTuple helper was in a Base-only branch, causing

```

julia> Core.eval(Core.Compiler, quote f(;a=1) = a end)
f (generic function with 1 method)

julia> Core.Compiler.f(;b=2)
ERROR: UndefVarError: `_new_NamedTuple` not defined
Stacktrace:
 [1] macro expansion
   @ Core.Compiler ./namedtuple.jl:0 [inlined]
 [2] structdiff(a::@NamedTuple{b::Int64}, b::Type{NamedTuple{(:a,)}})
   @ Core.Compiler ./namedtuple.jl:421
 [3] top-level scope
   @ REPL[2]:1
```

After this change, we have the expected
```
julia> Core.eval(Core.Compiler, quote f(;a=1) = a end)
f (generic function with 1 method)

julia> Core.Compiler.f(;b=2)
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching f(; b::Int64)

Closest candidates are:
  f(; a) got unsupported keyword argument "b"
   @ Core REPL[13]:1

Stacktrace:
 [1] kwerr(kw::@NamedTuple{b::Int64}, args::Function)
   @ Core.Compiler ./error.jl:165

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47150","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47150/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47150/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47150/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47150","id":1407077742,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM5T3k1u","number":47150,"title":"Inconsistent behaviour of `hasproperty` with Modules","user":{"login":"tecosaur","id":20903656,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwOTAzNjU2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20903656?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur","html_url":"https://github.com/tecosaur","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/tecosaur/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":423266620,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw0MjMyNjY2MjA=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/modules","name":"modules","color":"006b75","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":5,"created_at":"2022-10-13T03:20:50Z","updated_at":"2023-05-01T02:56:42Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"###### Julia 1.8.2, Linux

I feel like the behavior of `{has,get}{property,field}` is a bit suspect when it comes to Modules. The following example should be sufficiently illustrative.

```julia
julia> hasproperty(Main, :DataFrames)
false

julia> getproperty(Main, :DataFrames)
DataFrames

julia> hasfield(Main, :DataFrames)
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching hasfield(::Module, ::Symbol)
Closest candidates are:
  hasfield(::Type, ::Symbol) at reflection.jl:215
Stacktrace:
 [1] top-level scope
   @ REPL[26]:1

julia> getfield(Main, :DataFrames)
DataFrames
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47150/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47150/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1529283166","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47150#issuecomment-1529283166","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47150","id":1529283166,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5bJwJe","user":{"login":"oxinabox","id":5127634,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxMjc2MzQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5127634?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox","html_url":"https://github.com/oxinabox","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-05-01T02:56:42Z","updated_at":"2023-05-01T02:56:42Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Makes sense. "non base pipelines" we are making an OpaqueClosure with generated IR code.

The issue is that we want to optimize this IR after modifying it. So we completely change the function arguments, and then want to call `_ir_abstract_constant_propagation` to get rid of runtime dispatch. This makes "silly" assumptions like that the IR arguments match the MI arguments.

So this PR bypasses the argument processing so we can optimize the IR without having a matching MI.

I don't think this change should be merged as is, so putting it out there so someone more knowledgeable can figure out a better way to do this.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49384/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49384/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1529427927","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49384#issuecomment-1529427927","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49384","id":1529427927,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5bKTfX","user":{"login":"oxinabox","id":5127634,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxMjc2MzQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5127634?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox","html_url":"https://github.com/oxinabox","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-05-01T07:13:39Z","updated_at":"2023-05-01T07:13:39Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"To actually use this I needed to also add:

            ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49583/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49583/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1532429630","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49583#issuecomment-1532429630","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49583","id":1532429630,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5bVwU-","user":{"login":"oxinabox","id":5127634,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxMjc2MzQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5127634?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox","html_url":"https://github.com/oxinabox","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-05-03T03:59:45Z","updated_at":"2023-05-03T03:59:45Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"I think even not supporting `] dev`ed packages, but only the current active enviroment would be enough.
But regardless, we really should have something like this.


Here is some rough, untested code that might do it.
But it is slow because it uses `stacktrace()`
However maybe that's ok. If someone wants maximum speed, they can set `depwarn=none` 
rather than proposesed new default setting  of `--depwarn=mine` (or what ever we call it).
It is slow only when deprecations are hit, but the slowness doesn't depend on if it is there fault or not.

```julia
function is_author_file(path)
    # This might be tricked by stdlibs which seem to set file wrong in stacktraces
    
    # Put any directories we want to ignore here
    nonauthor_roots = joinpath.(Base.DEPOT_PATH, "packages")
    
    cur_path = path
    while ((cur_path=dirname(cur_path)) != "")
        dirname ∈ nonauthor_roots && return false
    end
    # coulnd't blame anyone else, must be our problem
    return true
end


function is_called_from_authors_code(log_emit_file)
    found_emit_file = false
    for frame in stacktrace()
        # Skip past helper code of logging system
        # with some thought and fragileness thsi could be removed and replaced with skipping first ~3 entries
        if !found_emit_file
            #not there yet
            if frame.file == log_emit_file
                found_emit_file = true
            end
            continue
        else
            # could insert logic here to handle deprecated 
            # methods called by other deprecated methods in the module
            # but skipping for now
            
            # Here we could be tricked by higher order functons
            # but skipping worrying about that for now
            # could put in a guard against at least the common ones like `map` and `sum` etc
            
            return is_author_file(frame.file)
        end
    end
    error("Failed to identify where function was used")
end

using LoggingExtras
ActiveFilteredLogger(global_logger()) do log
    log.group != :depwarn && return true  #pass nondepwarns straight on
    return is_called_from_authors_code(log.file)
end |> global_logger
```
I have never tested this code just putting it out there as a sketch of what the idea looks like really

This means it often breaks, due to changes in Pkg that are not mirrored in TestEnv yet.
Though for the last few releases the Pkg internals seem to be back in a period of stability.

I think at this point, TestEnv.jl has proven the utility of being able to activate the test enviroment. 
And that it would be reasonable to add `] activate --test` or similar.
If we did that and stdlib-ized the the TestEnv functionality then it wouldn't need 
Could keep TestEnv.jl around for compat reasons, but it could just delegate to Pkg if version is beyond 1.10

An alternative would be to expose just enough functionality to do this.
This probably means a public API of:
 - extract UUID from `[extras]` 
 -  easily get lists of packages from `target.test` 
 - and easily subset `[compat]`
 - Merge  Projects/Manifests","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3460/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3460/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

Though for the last few releases the Pkg internals seem to be back in a period of stability.

I think at this point, TestEnv.jl has proven the utility of being able to activate the test enviroment. 
And that it would be reasonable to add `] activate --test` or similar.
If we did that and stdlib-ized the the TestEnv functionality then it wouldn't need 
Could keep TestEnv.jl around for compat reasons, but it could just delegate to Pkg if version is beyond 1.10

An alternative would be to expose just enough functionality to do this.

 - extract UUID from `[extras]` 
 -  easily get lists of packages from `target.test` 
 - and easily subset `[compat]`

wanted","color":"128A0C","default":true,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2023-05-10T09:06:32Z","updated_at":"2023-05-11T17:09:50Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"`Pkg.why` currently just lists all the way to get to a dependency:

```
(@v1.9) pkg> why  Zlib_jll
  BinaryBuilder → BinaryBuilderBase → Bzip2_jll → Pkg → Downloads → LibCURL → LibCURL_jll → Zlib_jll
  BinaryBuilder → BinaryBuilderBase → CodecZlib → Zlib_jll
  BinaryBuilder → BinaryBuilderBase → Downloads → LibCURL → LibCURL_jll → Zlib_jll
  BinaryBuilder → BinaryBuilderBase → Gzip_jll → Pkg → Downloads → LibCURL → LibCURL_jll → Zlib_jll
  BinaryBuilder → BinaryBuilderBase → Pkg → Downloads → LibCURL → LibCURL_jll → Zlib_jll
  BinaryBuilder → BinaryBuilderBase → Tar_jll → Attr_jll → Pkg → Downloads → LibCURL → LibCURL_jll → Zlib_jll
  BinaryBuilder → BinaryBuilderBase → Tar_jll → Pkg → Downloads → LibCURL → LibCURL_jll → Zlib_jll
  BinaryBuilder → BinaryBuilderBase → XZ_jll → Pkg → Downloads → LibCURL → LibCURL_jll → Zlib_jll
  BinaryBuilder → BinaryBuilderBase → pigz_jll → Pkg → Downloads → LibCURL → LibCURL_jll → Zlib_jll
  BinaryBuilder → BinaryBuilderBase → pigz_jll → Zlib_jll
  BinaryBuilder → Downloads → LibCURL → LibCURL_jll → Zlib_jll
  BinaryBuilder → GitHub → HTTP → CodecZlib → Zlib_jll
  BinaryBuilder → GitHub → HTTP → OpenSSL → OpenSSL_jll → Pkg → Downloads → LibCURL → LibCURL_jll → Zlib_jll
  BinaryBuilder → GitHub → SodiumSeal → libsodium_jll → Pkg → Downloads → LibCURL → LibCURL_jll → Zlib_jll
  BinaryBuilder → HTTP → CodecZlib → Zlib_jll
```

However, this means that many paths are repeated (for example the `BinaryBuilder → BinaryBuilderBase` and `Pkg → Downloads → LibCURL → LibCURL_jll → Zlib_jll`) paths above.

It would be better to somehow avoid printing this all this repetition. I am not sure what the best way is to do this, maybe something like:

```
(@v1.9) pkg> why  Zlib_jll
  [1]: BinaryBuilder → BinaryBuilderBase
  [2]: Downloads →  LibCURL → LibCURL_jll → Zlib_jll

  [1] → Bzip2_jll → [2]
  [1] → CodecZlib → Zlib_jll
  [1] → [2]
  [1] → Gzip_jll → [2]
  [1] → Pkg -> [2]
  [1] → Tar_jll → Attr_jll → [2]
  [1] → Tar_jll → [2]
  [1] → XZ_jll → [2]
  [1] → pigz_jll → [2]
  [1] → pigz_jll → Zlib_jll
```

where the common paths are factored out (and then use some color coding to help guide the eye).

Someone who is interested in playing with this could try come up with a better UI.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3464/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3464/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/comments/1544376537","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3464#issuecomment-1544376537","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3464","id":1544376537,"node_id":"IC_kwDOBOhtSc5cDVDZ","user":{"login":"oxinabox","id":5127634,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxMjc2MzQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5127634?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox","html_url":"https://github.com/oxinabox","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-05-11T17:09:49Z","updated_at":"2023-05-11T17:09:49Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Beyond a tree you could draw the DAG.

I assume it's cos they want to work out if they can drop something from their dep chain.

The answer is probably No if they gave lots of independent paths.
But if the paths all share a few prefixes maybe the stuff on that prefix can be dropped as a dep, to get free.


We could keep current display as `why --full`  and then do something that identifies prefixes worth collapsing.
(And says "some paths were collapsed
 Use why --full to see) and

Maybe some rule which relates how deep a prefix is with how many paths it can have coming from it before it is collapsed. (Since otherwise things that were shallow would collapse too easily)
Say 
```
BinaryBuilder → BinaryBuilderBase → (via 10 paths) → Zlib_jll
  BinaryBuilder → GitHub → (via 4 paths)→ Zlib_jll
BinaryBuilder → Downloads → LibCURL → LibCURL_jll → Zlib_jll
  BinaryBuilder → HTTP → CodecZlib → Zlib_jll
```

So this let's user know that reducing there dependency would be. Best done if they can drop BinaryBuilderBase, and next best if they can drop GitHub.
(Though does anyone care about reducing if can't eliminate?)

However this kind of compression hids that 3 of the drps us via HTTP.jl at done point -- with different prefixes, and9 via CodecZlib.
But maybe user cares about them less since they are further up the chain from them so less likely to geva package they are able to modify?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/comments/1544408285/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

And I run into my tests failing because Const equality uses default equality, not the equality of the type it is a constant of.
Which is a problem for floats.
```julia
julia> Core.Const(0.0) == Core.Const(-0.0)
false
```

Perhaps we need to do it like this since, something might rely on it really being the same, not just mathematically equal.

base/compiler/ssair/)"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2023-05-18T21:40:09Z","updated_at":"2023-05-19T13:32:13Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":true,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/49882","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49882","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49882.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49882.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"This change extends our existing transformation for
 ```φ(a,b) === Const(c)   =>   φ(a === c, b === c)``` 
 to apply also to Core.ifelse calls, instead of just standard φ-Nodes

For a function `foo(cond) = isa(Core.ifelse(cond, 1.0, 1), Int64)` this improves the generated IR:

```julia
julia> @code_typed foo(true)
CodeInfo(
1 ─ %1 = Core.ifelse(cond, false, true)::Bool
└──      return %1
) => Bool
```

versus without this PR:
```julia
julia> @code_typed foo(true)
CodeInfo(
1 ─ %1 = Core.ifelse(cond, 1.0, 1)::Union{Float64, Int64}
│   %2 = (%1 isa Main.Int64)::Bool
└──      return %2
) => Bool
```

Draft to get early feedback before I give it another once-over and revise naming later. I'm not entirely happy with how a `Union{Expr,PhiNode}` traces through the code now, but it seemed preferable to duplicating the code

undoing #44358

I propose backporting this PR to bring the operation back in 1.9.1, and holding off #44358 til julia 2.0 where matching changes can be made as a whole set. cc: @antoine-levitt

#44358 was originally made with the assumption that noone was using this feature except in error.
Which was checked using PkgEval.
This was incorrect, and the check for some reason or another missed or skipped over ChainRules.jl.
This broke ChainRules.jl for anyone using reverse mode AD to go through `/` or `\`.

The use in ChainRules.jl is intentional and is required to achieve generality between different types.
Quoting @sethaxen  on slack

> This seems to be a direct consequence of how c = transpose(a)*b for vectors a and b produces a scalar, while c = permutedims(a)*b produces a vector. I'd argue the first case is convenient but wrong, but if we accept it, then since c/b is just the linear solve for a, then /(::Number, AbstractVector{<:Number}) should be supported.

The trivial fix to make ChainRules work again in 1.9.0 (https://github.com/JuliaDiff/ChainRules.jl/pull/718) is uglier, less performant and less numerically good.

I think we do not need to discuss if the feature in #44358 is desirable or not in this PR. 
Merely if it was too breaking or reverts commit 24d526871d5203317fa17f93f9a4ef9fb34e8632.
undoing #44358

I propose backporting this PR to bring the operation back in 1.9.1, and holding off #44358 til julia 2.0 where matching changes can be made as a whole set. cc: @antoine-levitt

#44358 was originally made with the assumption that noone was using this feature except in error.
Which was checked using PkgEval.
This was incorrect, and the check for some reason or another missed or skipped over ChainRules.jl.
This broke ChainRules.jl for anyone using reverse mode AD to go through `/` or `\`.

The use in ChainRules.jl is intentional and is required to achieve generality between different types.
Quoting @sethaxen  on slack

> This seems to be a direct consequence of how c = transpose(a)*b for vectors a and b produces a scalar, while c = permutedims(a)*b produces a vector. I'd argue the first case is convenient but wrong, but if we accept it, then since c/b is just the linear solve for a, then /(::Number, AbstractVector{<:Number}) should be supported.

The trivial fix to make ChainRules work again in 1.9.0 (https://github.com/JuliaDiff/ChainRules.jl/pull/718) is uglier, less performant and less numerically good.

I think we do not need to discuss if the feature in #44358 is desirable or not in this PR. 
Merely if it was too breaking or reverts commit 24d526871d5203317fa17f93f9a4ef9fb34e8632.
undoing #44358

I propose backporting this PR to bring the operation back in 1.9.1, and holding off #44358 til julia 2.0 where matching changes can be made as a whole set. cc: @antoine-levitt

#44358 was originally made with the assumption that noone was using this feature except in error.
Which was checked using PkgEval.
This was incorrect, and the check for some reason or another missed or skipped over ChainRules.jl.
This broke ChainRules.jl for anyone using reverse mode AD to go through `/` or `\`.

The use in ChainRules.jl is intentional and is required to achieve generality between different types.
Quoting @sethaxen  on slack

> This seems to be a direct consequence of how c = transpose(a)*b for vectors a and b produces a scalar, while c = permutedims(a)*b produces a vector. I'd argue the first case is convenient but wrong, but if we accept it, then since c/b is just the linear solve for a, then /(::Number, AbstractVector{<:Number}) should be supported.

The trivial fix to make ChainRules work again in 1.9.0 (https://github.com/JuliaDiff/ChainRules.jl/pull/718) is uglier, less performant and less numerically good.

I think we do not need to discuss if the feature in #44358 is desirable or not in this PR. 
Merely if it was too breaking or not.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49915/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49915/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1556722413","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49915#issuecomment-1556722413","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49915","id":1556722413,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5cybLt","user":{"login":"oxinabox","id":5127634,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxMjc2MzQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5127634?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox","html_url":"https://github.com/oxinabox","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-05-22T07:57:55Z","updated_at":"2023-05-22T07:57:55Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"> It's a bit concerning that pkgeval didn't catch this, maybe it got into chainrules after the pkgeval run?

base/compiler/ssair/)"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":5,"created_at":"2023-05-18T21:40:09Z","updated_at":"2023-05-23T11:57:33Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":true,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/49882","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49882","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49882.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49882.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"This change extends our existing transformation for
 ```φ(a,b) === Const(c)   =>   φ(a === c, b === c)``` 
 to apply also to Core.ifelse calls, instead of just standard φ-Nodes

For a function `foo(cond) = isa(Core.ifelse(cond, 1.0, 1), Int64)` this improves the generated IR:

```julia
julia> @code_typed foo(true)
CodeInfo(
1 ─ %1 = Core.ifelse(cond, false, true)::Bool
└──      return %1
) => Bool
```

versus without this PR:
```julia
julia> @code_typed foo(true)
CodeInfo(
1 ─ %1 = Core.ifelse(cond, 1.0, 1)::Union{Float64, Int64}
│   %2 = (%1 isa Main.Int64)::Bool
└──      return %2
) => Bool
```

Draft to get early feedback before I give it another once-over and revise naming later. I'm not entirely happy with how a `Union{Expr,PhiNode}` traces through the code now, but it seemed preferable to duplicating the code

call"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":14,"created_at":"2023-05-22T04:09:19Z","updated_at":"2023-05-24T11:18:17Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/49915","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49915","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49915.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49915.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"This reverts commit 24d526871d5203317fa17f93f9a4ef9fb34e8632.
undoing #44358

I propose backporting this PR to bring the operation back in 1.9.1, and holding off #44358 til julia 2.0 where matching changes can be made as a whole set. cc: @antoine-levitt

#44358 was originally made with the assumption that noone was using this feature except in error.
Which was checked using PkgEval.
This was incorrect, and the check for some reason or another missed or skipped over ChainRules.jl.
This broke ChainRules.jl for anyone using reverse mode AD to go through `/` or `\`.

The use in ChainRules.jl is intentional and is required to achieve generality between different types.
Quoting @sethaxen  on slack

> This seems to be a direct consequence of how c = transpose(a)*b for vectors a and b produces a scalar, while c = permutedims(a)*b produces a vector. I'd argue the first case is convenient but wrong, but if we accept it, then since c/b is just the linear solve for a, then /(::Number, AbstractVector{<:Number}) should be supported.

The trivial fix to make ChainRules work again in 1.9.0 (https://github.com/JuliaDiff/ChainRules.jl/pull/718) is uglier, less performant and less numerically good.

I think we do not need to discuss if the feature in #44358 is desirable or not in this PR. 
Merely if it was too breaking or not.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49915/reactions","total_count":3,"+1":2,"-1":1,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49915/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1560933834","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/49915#issuecomment-1560933834","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49915","id":1560933834,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5dCfXK","user":{"login":"oxinabox","id":5127634,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxMjc2MzQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5127634?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox","html_url":"https://github.com/oxinabox","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-05-24T11:18:16Z","updated_at":"2023-05-24T11:18:16Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Yes, deprecating it would be fine.

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3494","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3494/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3494/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3494/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3494","id":1723305724,"node_id":"I_kwDOBOhtSc5mt478","number":3494,"title":"Сompat does not include DEV version","user":{"login":"gryumov","id":33372067,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMzMzcyMDY3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/33372067?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gryumov","html_url":"https://github.com/gryumov","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gryumov/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gryumov/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gryumov/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gryumov/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gryumov/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gryumov/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gryumov/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gryumov/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gryumov/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2023-05-24T06:49:33Z","updated_at":"2023-05-25T06:10:35Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Сompat does not include DEV version

```toml
name = "Bar"
uuid = "26c37dcc-07de-4b08-afb5-6df828475b25"
authors = ["foo <foo@foo.com>"]
version = "0.1.0-dev"

[deps]
Dates = "ade2ca70-3891-5945-98fb-dc099432e06a"
Foo = "01ba1978-7efe-11ed-2893-03bfe3abf75a"

[compat]
Foo = "=0.5.0-dev"
julia = "1.8"
```

```julia
caused by: invalid version specifier: "0.5.0-dev"
Stacktrace:
  [1] error(s::String)
    @ Base ./error.jl:35
```

Moreover, if you specify without a DEV, then he will take this patch, what seems to be unsafe behavior

```
[compat]
Foo = "=0.5.0"
julia = "1.8"

Status `~/.julia/environments/v1.8/Project.toml`
  [26c37dcc] Foo =v0.5.0-dev
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3494/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3494/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/comments/1562332228","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3494#issuecomment-1562332228","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/3494","id":1562332228,"node_id":"IC_kwDOBOhtSc5dH0xE","user":{"login":"oxinabox","id":5127634,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUxMjc2MzQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5127634?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox","html_url":"https://github.com/oxinabox","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/oxinabox/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-05-25T06:10:35Z","updated_at":"2023-05-25T06:10:35Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Not supporting this is basically a feature.
The compat section of Project.toml will ignore pre-release info in things you have locally `]dev`ed's Project.toml's version fields
which means you can locally test that your package works with your downstream without cutting a release.

But you can't explictly depend on them to have that pulled from a registry, since at that point it's not a pre-release it's just a release.
The General registry has a CI check to make sure you aren't registering anything with a prerelease tag still on it.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/comments/1562332228/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
